actionable_feedback: Add an explicit 'Logic' sheet in greenhouse_control.xlsx listing the exact formula text for every derived column used in Zone_Analysis, Inputs_and_Assumptions, Decision_Table, and Sensitivity_Checks so reviewers and automated tools can read formulas without parsing generated OOXML.

Either correct or justify the sign and physical meaning of dehum_kWh_per_rh. If negative intentionally, add a one-line derivation or vendor citation in Inputs_and_Assumptions describing why dehumidification yields a negative energy delta in this modeling context.

Provide CSV exports (or an unzipped workbook XML snippet) of key computed columns (Zone_Analysis rows and Inputs_and_Assumptions) so graders without openpyxl can deterministically validate numeric formulas and results.

Add precise operational execution details in the memo: RACI (who executes / who verifies), pilot duration windows (e.g., 'apply dehumidify for up to 2 hours; monitor 15-min stress trend; reassess'), and explicit rollback thresholds and observation intervals.
Include short derivations or citations for heat_kWh_per_deg and co2_kwh_per_ppm similar to the dehumidification justification to remove ambiguity about coefficient provenance.

feedback_summary: Baseline deliverables meet the artifact contract and provide clear operational guidance. Two minor issues reduce confidence in fully automated verification and semantic correctness: (1) the dehumidification energy coefficient (dehum_kWh_per_rh = -0.004) is counterintuitive and may be a sign error or require explicit justification; (2) the evaluation environment could not programmatically inspect xlsx formulas (openpyxl/xlsxwriter unavailable), so formula correctness relies on the memo and trace. Addressing these items will improve trust, verifiability, and operational readiness.
